citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Daily observation of two rabbits to assess their health and well-being is not being done adequately, nor is there direct and
frequent communication with the attending veterinarian regarding their health problems. Two rabbits, identified during
previous inspections with animal welfare concerns, were found with progression of unresolved medical issues, as well as
new issues that have not been noticed by the licensee, and have not been re-evaluated by a veterinarian.
• The tan and white rabbit was again found with its right ear dropped, and the entire inner surface coated with thick,
layered flaky gray material. This material now extends below the ear onto the head. The skin around the thick material
along the edges of the ear are ulcerated exposing red, inflamed tissue. One area along the lower edge of the right ear
(approximately 1 inch long by 1/4 “ wide) near the ear canal, was actively oozing blood. The entire ear appeared
macerated, shrunken, misshapen, and irregular, with marked thickening around the ear canal opening. The left ear canal
now also has red skin around a similarly thick layered gray crusty material extending from the ear canal to about halfway
up the ear flap.
• The tan rabbit’s eye had improved, with both eyes open and only mild clear to white discharge at the corner of the left
eye with some pink skin surrounding the eye. However, this rabbit continued to have wet, matted fur over most of its lower
back with dry, flaky skin. A large area of dark brown, sticky material was adhered to the fur in this region. This rabbit is
occasionally shaking its head and has an overall unkempt appearance. Additionally, both ear canals now contained thick,
flaky gray material.
Despite the apparent worsening condition of one rabbit and the new condition in the other, the licensee stated the rabbits
were doing better since being seen by the attending veterinarian following the last inspection and no follow-up
appointments were scheduled or needed. Failure to identify new as well as the worsening of pre-existing health issues
and failing to maintain timely communication about those findings with the attending veterinarian can lead to treatment
delay, which may negatively impact the health and well-being of the animals.
Each exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation of all
animals to assess their health and well-being as well as direct and frequent communication so that timely and accurate
information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
Additional Inspectors:
STEPHANIE MAYARD, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct Repeat
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
Daily observation of two rabbits to assess their health and well-being is not being done adequately, nor is there direct and 
frequent communication with the attending veterinarian regarding their health problems. Two rabbits, identified during 
previous inspections with animal welfare concerns, were found with progression of unresolved medical issues, as well as 
new issues that have not been noticed by the licensee, and have not been re-evaluated by a veterinarian. 
 
• The tan and white rabbit was again found with its right ear dropped, and the entire inner surface coated with thick, 
layered flaky gray material. This material now extends below the ear onto the head. The skin around the thick material 
along the edges of the ear are ulcerated exposing red, inflamed tissue. One area along the lower edge of the right ear 
(approximately 1 inch long by 1/4 “ wide) near the ear canal, was actively oozing blood. The entire ear appeared 
macerated, shrunken, misshapen, and irregular, with marked thickening around the ear canal opening. The left ear canal 
now also has red skin around a similarly thick layered gray crusty material extending from the ear canal to about halfway

• The tan rabbit’s eye had improved, with both eyes open and only mild clear to white discharge at the corner of the left

Despite the apparent worsening condition of one rabbit and the new condition in the other, the licensee stated the rabbits 
were doing better since being seen by the attending veterinarian following the last inspection and no follow-up 
appointments were scheduled or needed. Failure to identify new as well as the worsening of pre-existing health issues 
and failing to maintain timely communication about those findings with the attending veterinarian can lead to treatment

Each exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation of all 
animals to assess their health and well-being as well as direct and frequent communication so that timely and accurate 
information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
STEPHANIE MAYARD, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
